title: Raw-Categorical CatBoost Blend Instead Of More Manual Frequency Features
summary: Your strongest solutions still handcraft many sparse interactions like `Race_Compound` and `Driver_Compound`.
rationale: Your strongest solutions still handcraft many sparse interactions like `Race_Compound` and `Driver_Compound`. CatBoost’s ordered categorical statistics are specifically designed to extract signal from high-cardinality categories and combinations while reducing target leakage relative to naive encodings.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoostClassifier on mostly raw columns plus a limited set of numeric physics features, keeping `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, `Stint`, and a few interaction strings as categorical. Use race-grouped folds and causal row ordering within each driver-race sequence for OOF predictions, then blend with the current tree model.
expected_effect: Captures sparse entity interactions and conditional priors more efficiently than additional manual frequency/median encodings.
risk: If folds are not grouped correctly, ordered stats can still look better locally than they generalize; CatBoost may also underperform if numeric heuristics already dominate.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
